title: Philippine CPA Board Exam Review - Financial Accounting and Reporting (FAR)
description:
Complete review course for the Philippine CPA Board Exam FAR section. This comprehensive course covers all major topics tested in the board exam including conceptual framework, assets, liabilities, equity, and financial statement analysis.

**Course Features:**
• 72+ hours of video lectures
• 200+ practice questions with detailed explanations
• 6 comprehensive board exam simulations
• Downloadable PDF resources and templates
• Progress tracking and performance analytics
• Mobile-friendly learning platform
• Lifetime access to course materials

**What You'll Learn:**
✓ Master PFRS applications for financial statement preparation
✓ Analyze complex accounting transactions
✓ Prepare consolidated financial statements
✓ Apply revenue recognition principles
✓ Account for PPE, intangible assets, and investment property
✓ Implement proper accounting for income taxes and employee benefits
✓ Prepare statement of cash flows
✓ Navigate ethical considerations in financial reporting

**Target Audience:**
• Accounting graduates preparing for the CPA Board Exam
• Accountants seeking to strengthen PFRS knowledge
• Finance professionals transitioning to accounting roles
• Review center students seeking supplementary materials
• Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) preparing for return to Philippine practice
shortDescription: Complete 72-hour FAR review course with 200+ practice questions, board exam simulations, and comprehensive resources for Philippine CPA Board Exam preparation.
requirements: Access to accounting calculator or spreadsheet software
Dedication to study 2-3 hours per day
whatYouLearn: Master PFRS applications for financial statement preparation under Philippine setting
Analyze complex accounting transactions including business combinations, leases, and financial instruments
Prepare consolidated financial statements for parent-subsidiary relationships
Apply revenue recognition principles under PFRS 15
Account for property, plant and equipment, intangible assets, and investment property
Implement proper accounting for income taxes, employee benefits, and provisions
Prepare statement of cash flows using both direct and indirect methods
Navigate ethical considerations and professional responsibilities in financial reporting
Develop exam-taking strategies for the CPA Board Exam FAR section
Build confidence through timed practice exams and performance analytics
Solve 200+ practice problems with detailed step-by-step solutions
Master time management for the 4-hour board exam
Identify common exam traps and avoid costly mistakes
Build a personal formula sheet and quick reference guide
targetAudience: Accounting graduates preparing for the CPA Board Exam
Accountants seeking to strengthen PFRS knowledge
Finance professionals transitioning to accounting roles
Review center students seeking supplementary materials
Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) preparing for return to Philippine practice
Working professionals with limited study time
Students who want comprehensive practice with instant feedback

sections.title: Section 1: Conceptual Framework and Philippine Financial Reporting Standards
sections.description: Foundation of financial reporting under PFRS including conceptual framework, qualitative characteristics, and standard-setting process

lessons.title: Lesson 1.1: Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting
lessons.description:
**Learning Objectives:**
• Understand the purpose and components of the Conceptual Framework
• Identify the qualitative characteristics of useful financial information
• Apply the concepts of recognition, measurement, and presentation
• Distinguish between capital maintenance concepts

**Key Topics:**
1. Objective of General Purpose Financial Reporting
2. Qualitative Characteristics of Useful Financial Information
   - Fundamental: Relevance, Faithful Representation
   - Enhancing: Comparability, Verifiability, Timeliness, Understandability
3. Financial Statements and the Reporting Entity
4. Elements of Financial Statements
5. Recognition and Derecognition
6. Measurement
7. Presentation and Disclosure
8. Concepts of Capital and Capital Maintenance

lessons.title: Lesson 1.2: Philippine Financial Reporting Standards (PFRS) Overview
lessons.description:
**Learning Objectives:**
• Understand the structure of PFRS in the Philippines
• Identify the differences between PFRS and IFRS
• Navigate the standard-setting process
• Apply effective dates of standards

**Key Topics:**
1. History of Accounting Standards in the Philippines
2. Financial Reporting Standards Council (FRSC)
3. Philippine Interpretations Committee
4. PFRS vs IFRS: Key Differences
5. Effective Dates and Transition Provisions
6. First-time Adoption of PFRS (PFRS 1)
7. Small and Medium-sized Entity Framework
8. Recent Updates and Exposure Drafts

lessons.title: Lesson 1.3: Presentation of Financial Statements (PFRS 1/IAS 1)
lessons.description:
**Learning Objectives:**
• Prepare a complete set of financial statements
• Apply minimum line item requirements
• Understand current/non-current classification
• Apply presentation and disclosure requirements

**Key Topics:**
1. Objective and Scope of PFRS 1
2. Complete Set of Financial Statements
   - Statement of Financial Position
   - Statement of Comprehensive Income
   - Statement of Changes in Equity
   - Statement of Cash Flows
   - Notes to Financial Statements
3. Current vs Non-Current Classification
4. Offsetting
5. Comparative Information
6. Materiality and Aggregation
7. Going Concern Assessment
8. Events After Reporting Period

lessons.title: Lesson 1.4: Accounting Policies, Changes in Estimates and Errors (PFRS 8/IAS 8)
lessons.description:
**Learning Objectives:**
• Select and apply accounting policies
• Account for changes in accounting policies
• Account for changes in accounting estimates
• Correct prior period errors

**Key Topics:**
1. Selection of Accounting Policies
2. Changes in Accounting Policies
   - Retrospective Application
   - Impracticability Exception
3. Changes in Accounting Estimates
   - Prospective Application
4. Prior Period Errors
   - Retrospective Restatement
5. Disclosure Requirements
6. Interaction with Other Standards
7. Practice Problems and Examples

sections.title: Section 2: Assets - Current and Non-Current
sections.description: Comprehensive coverage of asset recognition, measurement, and disclosure including cash, receivables, inventories, and non-current assets

lessons.title: Lesson 2.1: Cash and Cash Equivalents
lessons.description:
**Learning Objectives:**

5. Restricted Cash

9. Common Board Exam Problems

lessons.title: Lesson 2.2: Trade and Other Receivables
lessons.description:

5. Pledging of Receivables
6. Assigning of Receivables
7. Notes Receivable

8. Disclosure Requirements
9. Board Exam Problem Solving Techniques

lessons.title: Lesson 2.3: Inventories
lessons.description:

6. Agricultural Products
7. Disclosure Requirements
8. Detailed Worked Examples

lessons.description:
**Learning Objectives:**

**Simulation Details:**

**Topics Covered:**
• Conceptual Framework and PFRS
• Assets (Current and Non-Current)
• Liabilities and Provisions
• Equity and Revenue
• Financial Statement Analysis

resources.title: Top 100 Most Common Board Exam Questions

programs.title: Lesson 1.1: Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 1.1: Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting. Test your understanding with 10 questions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental qualitative characteristic of useful financial information according to the Conceptual Framework?
questions.explanation: The fundamental qualitative characteristics are Relevance and Faithful Representation. Comparability, Timeliness, Verifiability, and Understandability are enhancing qualitative characteristics.

questions.question: Financial information is relevant when it has:
options: Both predictive value and confirmatory value
questions.correctAnswer: Both predictive value and confirmatory value
questions.explanation: Relevant financial information must have both predictive value (helps predict future outcomes) and confirmatory value (provides feedback about previous evaluations).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an element of financial statements?
questions.explanation: The elements of financial statements are assets, liabilities, equity, income (revenue and gains), and expenses (including losses). Cash flows is a presentation element, not a statement of financial position element.

questions.question: According to the Conceptual Framework, an asset is:
options: A resource controlled by the entity as a result of past events
A present obligation of the entity arising from past events
The residual interest in the assets of the entity after deducting liabilities
Increases in economic benefits during the accounting period
questions.correctAnswer: A resource controlled by the entity as a result of past events
questions.explanation: An asset is a present economic resource controlled by the entity as a result of past events. An economic resource is a right that has the potential to produce economic benefits.

questions.question: The going concern assumption states that:
options: The entity will continue to operate for the foreseeable future
The entity will be liquidated in the near future
The entity will be sold to another party
The entity will change its business model
questions.correctAnswer: The entity will continue to operate for the foreseeable future
questions.explanation: The going concern assumption presumes that the entity will continue in operation for the foreseeable future and has neither the intention nor the need to liquidate or curtail materially the scale of its operations.

questions.question: Which measurement basis uses the amount of cash or cash equivalents that would have to be paid if the same or an equivalent asset were acquired currently?
questions.explanation: Current cost is the amount of cash or cash equivalents that would have to be paid if the same or an equivalent asset were acquired currently.

questions.question: Faithful representation requires that financial information be:
options: Complete, neutral, and free from error
questions.correctAnswer: Complete, neutral, and free from error
questions.explanation: To be a perfectly faithful representation, a depiction must have three characteristics: complete, neutral, and free from error.

questions.question: The Conceptual Framework identifies capital maintenance concepts. Under physical capital maintenance:
options: Profit is the increase in nominal money capital
Profit is the increase in operating capability
All gains are recognized in profit or loss
questions.correctAnswer: Profit is the increase in operating capability
questions.explanation: Under physical capital maintenance, profit is the increase in physical productive capacity (operating capability) over the period.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an expense?
options: Depreciation of property, plant and equipment
questions.correctAnswer: Depreciation of property, plant and equipment
questions.explanation: Depreciation is an expense as it represents a decrease in economic benefits during the accounting period. Payment of dividends, purchase of inventory, and loan repayment are not expenses.

questions.question: According to the Conceptual Framework, when should an entity derecognize an asset?
options: When the entity loses control of the asset
When the asset is fully depreciated
When the asset is sold at a loss
When the asset's carrying amount is impaired
questions.correctAnswer: When the entity loses control of the asset
questions.explanation: An entity derecognizes an asset when it loses control of the asset (all or part). This could be through sale, expiration, or when the asset no longer meets the definition of an asset.

programs.title: Lesson 1.2: Philippine Financial Reporting Standards (PFRS) Overview - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 1.2: Philippine Financial Reporting Standards (PFRS) Overview. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Which body is responsible for formulating accounting standards in the Philippines?
questions.explanation: The FRSC is responsible for establishing and improving accounting standards in the Philippines that are generally accepted and promulgated by the Board of Accountancy.

questions.question: PFRS 1 deals with:
options: First-time Adoption of Philippine Financial Reporting Standards
questions.correctAnswer: First-time Adoption of Philippine Financial Reporting Standards
questions.explanation: PFRS 1 prescribes the procedures that an entity must follow when it adopts PFRSs as the basis for preparing its general purpose financial statements for the first time.

questions.question: Under PFRS for Small and Medium-sized Entities (SMEs), which of the following is simplified?
questions.explanation: Under PFRS for SMEs, borrowing costs are expensed when incurred rather than capitalized to qualifying assets, unlike full PFRS where borrowing costs must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about PFRS convergence with IFRS is TRUE?
options: PFRS are word-for-word identical to IFRS
PFRS may have different effective dates than IFRS
PFRS are completely independent from IFRS
PFRS do not require any local adaptations
questions.correctAnswer: PFRS may have different effective dates than IFRS
questions.explanation: While PFRS are generally converged with IFRS, they may have different effective dates and some local adaptations to suit Philippine conditions.

questions.question: The Philippine Interpretations Committee is responsible for:
options: Providing guidance on the application of PFRS
questions.correctAnswer: Providing guidance on the application of PFRS
questions.explanation: The Philippine Interpretations Committee provides timely guidance on accounting issues that arise in applying PFRS through the issuance of Philippine Interpretations.

programs.title: Lesson 1.3: Presentation of Financial Statements (PFRS 1/IAS 1) - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 1.3: Presentation of Financial Statements (PFRS 1/IAS 1). Test your understanding with 6 questions.

questions.question: Under PFRS 1, a complete set of financial statements includes all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: A complete set of financial statements includes a statement of financial position, statement of comprehensive income, statement of changes in equity, statement of cash flows, and notes. Statement of retained earnings is not a required statement under PFRS.

questions.question: Current assets include all of the following EXCEPT:
options: Assets expected to be realized within 12 months
Assets held primarily for trading purposes
questions.explanation: Current assets are assets expected to be realized within 12 months or the normal operating cycle, held primarily for trading, or cash. Property, plant and equipment are non-current assets.

questions.question: According to PFRS 1, when should an entity present a statement of financial position as at the beginning of the preceding period?
options: Always, as part of every set of financial statements
When it applies an accounting policy retrospectively or makes a retrospective restatement
Never, as PFRS does not require it
questions.correctAnswer: When it applies an accounting policy retrospectively or makes a retrospective restatement
questions.explanation: An entity must present a third statement of financial position as at the beginning of the preceding period when it applies an accounting policy retrospectively, makes a retrospective restatement, or reclassifies items.

questions.question: The statement of comprehensive income can be presented as:
options: Either a single statement or two separate statements
A note to the financial statements
questions.correctAnswer: Either a single statement or two separate statements
questions.explanation: PFRS 1 allows an entity to present profit or loss and other comprehensive income in either a single statement of comprehensive income or in two separate statements.

questions.question: Under PFRS 1, which of the following is NOT a minimum line item for the statement of financial position?
questions.explanation: Earnings per share is a minimum line item for the statement of comprehensive income, not the statement of financial position.

questions.question: Materiality in the context of PFRS 1 means:
options: Items must be presented separately if they are individually significant
Immaterial items can be aggregated with similar items
Both A and B are correct
Only items above ₱100,000 need to be disclosed
questions.correctAnswer: Both A and B are correct
questions.explanation: Materiality requires that each material class of similar items be presented separately, while immaterial amounts can be aggregated with items of a similar nature or function.

programs.title: Lesson 1.4: Accounting Policies, Changes in Estimates and Errors (PFRS 8/IAS 8) - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 1.4: Accounting Policies, Changes in Estimates and Errors (PFRS 8/IAS 8). Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: When an entity changes an accounting policy, it should apply the change:
options: Never, accounting policies cannot be changed
questions.explanation: A change in accounting policy should be applied retrospectively by adjusting opening retained earnings and comparative information, unless it is impracticable to do so.

questions.question: A change in accounting estimate is accounted for:
questions.explanation: A change in accounting estimate is accounted for prospectively by including the effects in profit or loss of the current period and future periods affected.

questions.question: Prior period errors should be corrected by:
options: Adjusting the opening balance of assets and liabilities
Restating comparative amounts for the prior period
Both A and B are required
Recognizing in current period profit or loss
questions.correctAnswer: Both A and B are required
questions.explanation: Prior period errors are corrected retrospectively by restating comparative amounts for the prior period and adjusting the opening balance of assets, liabilities, and equity.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a change in accounting estimate?
options: Changing from FIFO to weighted average for inventory
Changing the depreciation method from straight-line to reducing balance
Correcting an error in calculating depreciation
questions.correctAnswer: Changing the depreciation method from straight-line to reducing balance
questions.explanation: Changing the depreciation method is a change in accounting estimate (as depreciation method reflects the pattern of consumption of future economic benefits), not a change in accounting policy.

questions.question: Under IAS 8, when is retrospective application considered impracticable?
options: When it is too costly to apply
When applying it requires assumptions about management's intent in a prior period
When the entity has insufficient staff
questions.correctAnswer: When applying it requires assumptions about management's intent in a prior period
questions.explanation: Retrospective application is impracticable when applying it requires assumptions about what management's intent would have been in that period, or when it requires significant estimates for which necessary information is not available.

programs.title: Lesson 2.1: Cash and Cash Equivalents - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 2.1: Cash and Cash Equivalents. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT considered cash?
questions.explanation: Post-dated checks are not considered cash because they cannot be used to settle obligations until the date on the check. They are classified as receivables.

questions.question: Cash equivalents include:
options: Highly liquid investments with maturity of 3 months or less
questions.correctAnswer: Highly liquid investments with maturity of 3 months or less
questions.explanation: Cash equivalents are short-term, highly liquid investments that are readily convertible to known amounts of cash and are subject to an insignificant risk of changes in value (typically 3 months or less maturity).

questions.question: In a bank reconciliation, deposits in transit should be:
options: Added to the bank statement balance
Subtracted from the bank statement balance
questions.correctAnswer: Added to the bank statement balance
questions.explanation: Deposits in transit (outstanding deposits) are amounts received and recorded by the company but not yet recorded by the bank. They should be added to the bank statement balance.

questions.question: The imprest system for petty cash means:
options: The petty cash fund is replenished to a fixed amount
Any amount can be taken from petty cash
Petty cash is used only for large expenses
questions.correctAnswer: The petty cash fund is replenished to a fixed amount
questions.explanation: Under the imprest system, the petty cash fund is established at a fixed amount and replenished back to that fixed amount periodically based on the expenses paid.

questions.question: Bank service charges appearing on the bank statement but not yet recorded in the books should be:
options: Added to the bank statement balance
questions.explanation: Bank service charges are deducted by the bank but not yet recorded by the company. They should be subtracted from the book balance in the bank reconciliation.

programs.title: Lesson 2.3: Inventories - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 2.3: Inventories. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Under PFRS 2, which inventory costing method is NOT permitted?
questions.explanation: LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) is not permitted under PFRS 2/IAS 2 because it does not reflect the actual flow of goods in most cases and can result in obsolete inventory valuations.

questions.question: Net realizable value (NRV) is:
options: The estimated selling price in the ordinary course of business
The estimated selling price less estimated costs of completion and sale
questions.correctAnswer: The estimated selling price less estimated costs of completion and sale
questions.explanation: NRV is the estimated selling price in the ordinary course of business less the estimated costs of completion and the estimated costs necessary to make the sale.

questions.question: When inventory is written down to NRV, the loss should be recognized:
options: As a separate line item in profit or loss
As part of cost of goods sold
questions.correctAnswer: As a separate line item in profit or loss
questions.explanation: When inventory is written down to NRV, the write-down is recognized as an expense in profit or loss, typically as a separate line item or as part of cost of goods sold with disclosure.

questions.question: Under the weighted average cost formula, the cost of inventory is determined by:
options: Using the cost of the earliest purchases
Using the cost of the most recent purchases
Dividing the total cost of goods available for sale by total units available
Using the specific cost of each item
questions.correctAnswer: Dividing the total cost of goods available for sale by total units available
questions.explanation: Under weighted average, the cost of inventory is determined by dividing the total cost of goods available for sale by the total number of units available for sale.

questions.question: Which of the following costs should NOT be included in the cost of inventories?
questions.explanation: Administrative overheads not related to bringing inventories to their present location and condition are not included in inventory cost. They are expensed as incurred.

programs.title: Lesson 2.6: Property, Plant and Equipment - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 2.6: Property, Plant and Equipment. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Under PFRS 16, which of the following should be recognized as part of the cost of property, plant and equipment?
options: Costs of conducting business in a new location
Administration and other general overhead costs
Costs of opening a new facility
questions.explanation: Initial delivery and handling costs are directly attributable costs that bring the asset to the location and condition necessary for it to be capable of operating in the manner intended by management.

questions.question: The revaluation model under PFRS 16:
options: Is not permitted for property, plant and equipment
Requires assets to be carried at fair value at the date of revaluation
Can only be applied to land and buildings
questions.correctAnswer: Requires assets to be carried at fair value at the date of revaluation
questions.explanation: Under the revaluation model, an asset is carried at a revalued amount, being its fair value at the date of revaluation less subsequent depreciation and impairment losses.

questions.question: When an asset is revalued upward, the increase should be recognized:
options: In other comprehensive income as a revaluation surplus
questions.correctAnswer: In other comprehensive income as a revaluation surplus
questions.explanation: A revaluation increase is credited directly to equity under the heading revaluation surplus, unless it reverses a revaluation decrease of the same asset previously recognized as an expense.

questions.question: Depreciation of an asset begins when:
options: The asset is available for use
The asset is first used in production
questions.correctAnswer: The asset is available for use
questions.explanation: Depreciation begins when the asset is available for use, meaning when it is in the location and condition necessary for it to be capable of operating in the manner intended by management.

questions.question: Which depreciation method allocates a higher amount of depreciation in the earlier years of an asset's life?
questions.explanation: The declining balance method applies a constant percentage to the carrying amount, resulting in higher depreciation in earlier years and decreasing amounts in later years.

programs.title: Lesson 2.7: Intangible Assets and Goodwill - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 2.7: Intangible Assets and Goodwill. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a criterion for recognizing an internally generated intangible asset?
options: Technical feasibility of completing the asset
Intention to complete and use the asset
Ability to use or sell the asset
All costs can be measured reliably during the research phase
questions.correctAnswer: All costs can be measured reliably during the research phase
questions.explanation: Research phase costs must be expensed as incurred. It is only during the development phase that costs can be capitalized if all recognition criteria are met.

questions.question: Internally generated goodwill is:
questions.explanation: Internally generated goodwill is not recognized as an asset because it is not an identifiable resource controlled by the entity that can be measured reliably.

questions.question: An intangible asset with an indefinite useful life:
options: Is amortized over its legal life
Is not amortized but tested for impairment annually
questions.correctAnswer: Is not amortized but tested for impairment annually
questions.explanation: An intangible asset with an indefinite useful life is not amortized. Instead, it is tested for impairment annually and whenever there is an indication of impairment.

questions.question: Which of the following can be recognized as an intangible asset?
questions.explanation: Internally generated brands, mastheads, customer lists, and similar items cannot be recognized as intangible assets. Only separately acquired intangible assets can be recognized.

questions.question: Goodwill acquired in a business combination represents:
options: The excess of the cost of the combination over the acquirer's interest in the net fair value of the acquiree's identifiable assets and liabilities
The excess of the acquiree's net assets over the purchase price
The difference between the book value and fair value of net assets
The present value of future cash flows
questions.correctAnswer: The excess of the cost of the combination over the acquirer's interest in the net fair value of the acquiree's identifiable assets and liabilities
questions.explanation: Goodwill is measured as the excess of the consideration transferred over the net identifiable assets acquired. It represents future economic benefits arising from assets that are not individually identified.

programs.title: Lesson 2.8: Impairment of Assets - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 2.8: Impairment of Assets. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: An asset is impaired when:
options: Its carrying amount exceeds its recoverable amount
Its recoverable amount exceeds its carrying amount
questions.correctAnswer: Its carrying amount exceeds its recoverable amount
questions.explanation: An asset is impaired when its carrying amount exceeds its recoverable amount. The recoverable amount is the higher of fair value less costs of disposal and value in use.

questions.question: Value in use is:
options: The fair value of the asset
The present value of future cash flows expected from the asset
The replacement cost of the asset
The historical cost of the asset
questions.correctAnswer: The present value of future cash flows expected from the asset
questions.explanation: Value in use is the present value of the future cash flows expected to be derived from an asset or cash-generating unit.

questions.question: A reversal of an impairment loss on a revalued asset should be recognized:
options: As a reduction of the original impairment loss
questions.explanation: A reversal of an impairment loss on a revalued asset is recognized in other comprehensive income and increases the revaluation surplus, unless it exceeds the carrying amount that would have been determined.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an indicator of potential impairment?
options: Adverse changes in the technological environment
Expected future cash flows exceeding budget
questions.correctAnswer: Expected future cash flows exceeding budget
questions.explanation: An increase in expected future cash flows is not an indicator of impairment. External indicators of impairment include market value declines and adverse changes in the business environment.

questions.question: Goodwill acquired in a business combination should be:
questions.explanation: Goodwill is not amortized but must be tested for impairment annually and whenever there is an indication of impairment. It is allocated to cash-generating units.

programs.title: Lesson 3.1: Financial Liabilities - Basic and Compound - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 3.1: Financial Liabilities - Basic and Compound. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: When bonds are issued at a premium, the effective interest rate is:
questions.explanation: When bonds are issued at a premium, investors pay more than face value because the stated interest rate is higher than market rates, resulting in a lower effective interest rate.

questions.question: Under the effective interest method, interest expense is calculated as:
questions.explanation: Interest expense under the effective interest method is calculated by multiplying the carrying amount of the financial liability by the effective interest rate.

questions.question: When bonds are retired before maturity, any gain or loss is recognized:
questions.explanation: When bonds are retired before maturity, the difference between the carrying amount and the redemption price is recognized as a gain or loss in profit or loss.

questions.question: For a bond issued at a discount, interest expense each period will:
questions.explanation: For a bond issued at a discount, the carrying amount increases over time as the discount is amortized, resulting in increasing interest expense (carrying amount × effective rate).

questions.question: A convertible bond is classified as:
options: A compound financial instrument with debt and equity components
questions.correctAnswer: A compound financial instrument with debt and equity components
questions.explanation: A convertible bond contains both a debt component (the bond) and an equity component (the conversion option). These must be separated and classified accordingly.

programs.title: Lesson 3.4: Income Taxes - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 3.4: Income Taxes. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Deferred tax liabilities arise from:
questions.explanation: Deferred tax liabilities arise from taxable temporary differences - differences between the carrying amount of an asset or liability and its tax base that will result in taxable amounts in future periods.

questions.question: Which of the following creates a deferred tax asset?
options: Estimated warranty expense not yet deductible for tax
Development costs capitalized for accounting but expensed for tax
questions.correctAnswer: Estimated warranty expense not yet deductible for tax
questions.explanation: Estimated warranty expense creates a deductible temporary difference (recognized for accounting but not yet deductible for tax), resulting in a deferred tax asset.

questions.question: The tax base of an asset is:
options: The amount that will be deductible for tax purposes
The amount that is attributable to the asset for tax purposes
The carrying amount in the financial statements
The fair value of the asset
questions.correctAnswer: The amount that will be deductible for tax purposes
questions.explanation: The tax base of an asset is the amount that will be deductible for tax purposes against any taxable economic benefits that will flow to the entity when it recovers the carrying amount of the asset.

questions.question: Which of the following is a permanent difference?
options: Depreciation using different methods for accounting and tax
Warranty expense recognized differently for accounting and tax
questions.explanation: Non-deductible penalties and fines are permanent differences because they will never be deductible for tax purposes, unlike temporary differences which will reverse.

questions.question: Deferred tax should be measured using:
options: The rate expected to apply when the asset is realized or liability settled
The rate at the end of the previous period
An average rate over the last 5 years
questions.correctAnswer: The rate expected to apply when the asset is realized or liability settled
questions.explanation: Deferred tax assets and liabilities are measured at the tax rates that are expected to apply to the period when the asset is realized or the liability is settled.

programs.title: Lesson 3.5: Revenue from Contracts with Customers - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 3.5: Revenue from Contracts with Customers. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the five steps in PFRS 15?
options: Recognize revenue when cash is received
Allocate the transaction price to performance obligations
questions.correctAnswer: Recognize revenue when cash is received
questions.explanation: The five steps are: identify the contract, identify performance obligations, determine transaction price, allocate transaction price, and recognize revenue when/as performance obligations are satisfied.

questions.question: A performance obligation is a promise to transfer:
questions.explanation: A performance obligation is a promise in a contract to transfer to the customer either a good or service (or bundle of goods or services) that is distinct.

questions.question: Variable consideration should be included in the transaction price to the extent that:
options: It is probable that a significant reversal will not occur
The customer has paid the variable amount
The amount can be estimated with certainty
questions.correctAnswer: It is probable that a significant reversal will not occur
questions.explanation: Variable consideration is included in the transaction price only to the extent that it is highly probable that a significant reversal in the amount of cumulative revenue recognized will not occur.

questions.question: Revenue is recognized over time when:
options: The customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits
The entity's performance creates an asset with no alternative use
The entity's performance does not create an asset and has right to payment
Any of the above criteria are met
questions.correctAnswer: Any of the above criteria are met
questions.explanation: Revenue is recognized over time if any of these criteria are met: simultaneous receipt and consumption, no alternative use with right to payment, or no alternative use with no enforceable right.

questions.question: The standalone selling price of a good or service is:
options: The price at which the entity would sell the good or service separately
The price the customer is willing to pay
questions.correctAnswer: The price at which the entity would sell the good or service separately
questions.explanation: The standalone selling price is the price at which the entity would sell a promised good or service separately to a customer.

programs.title: Lesson 4.1: Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 4.1: Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: The functional currency is:
options: Always the currency of the country where the entity is located
The currency of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates
The currency in which the financial statements are presented
The currency of the parent company
questions.correctAnswer: The currency of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates
questions.explanation: The functional currency is the currency of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates. It is determined by considering various factors such as the currency that mainly influences sales prices.

questions.question: When translating foreign currency transactions, which exchange rate should be used for non-monetary items carried at historical cost?
options: Closing rate at the end of the reporting period
Historical rate at the date of the transaction
Spot rate at the date of translation
questions.correctAnswer: Historical rate at the date of the transaction
questions.explanation: Non-monetary items carried at historical cost should be translated using the exchange rate at the date of the transaction.

questions.question: Exchange differences arising on monetary items are recognized:
options: As an adjustment to the carrying amount
questions.explanation: Exchange differences on monetary items are recognized in profit or loss in the period in which they arise, except for differences on monetary items forming part of a net investment.

questions.question: When translating the financial statements of a foreign operation, the closing rate is used for:
questions.explanation: Assets and liabilities of a foreign operation are translated at the closing rate at the date of the statement of financial position.

questions.question: The functional currency of an entity cannot be changed:
options: Once determined, it can never be changed
Unless there a change in the underlying transactions, events, and conditions
If it differs from the parent's currency
questions.correctAnswer: Unless there a change in the underlying transactions, events, and conditions
questions.explanation: The functional currency is not changed unless there is a change in the underlying transactions, events, and conditions that determined it initially.

programs.title: Lesson 4.3: Business Combinations - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 4.3: Business Combinations. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Under PFRS 3, which method must be used to account for business combinations?
questions.explanation: PFRS 3 requires the use of the acquisition method for all business combinations, which involves identifying the acquirer, determining the acquisition date, recognizing and measuring assets and liabilities.

questions.question: Goodwill in a business combination is calculated as:
options: Consideration transferred + Non-controlling interest - Net identifiable assets acquired
Consideration transferred - Non-controlling interest + Net identifiable assets acquired
Net identifiable assets acquired - Consideration transferred
Fair value of acquiree - Book value of acquiree
questions.correctAnswer: Consideration transferred + Non-controlling interest - Net identifiable assets acquired
questions.explanation: Goodwill = Consideration transferred + Amount of non-controlling interest + Fair value of previously held interest - Net identifiable assets acquired.

questions.question: A bargain purchase gain occurs when:
options: The acquirer pays more than the fair value of net identifiable assets
The acquirer pays less than the fair value of net identifiable assets
questions.correctAnswer: The acquirer pays less than the fair value of net identifiable assets
questions.explanation: A bargain purchase occurs when the net identifiable assets acquired exceed the total consideration plus non-controlling interest. The gain is recognized in profit or loss.

questions.question: The acquisition date is:
options: The date the contract is signed
The date the acquirer obtains control of the acquiree
The date of the first payment
questions.correctAnswer: The date the acquirer obtains control of the acquiree
questions.explanation: The acquisition date is the date on which the acquirer obtains control of the acquiree, which is typically the closing date.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT recognized separately from goodwill in a business combination?
questions.explanation: Pre-existing relationships are not recognized separately from goodwill as they do not meet the definition of an asset of the acquiree at the acquisition date.

programs.title: Lesson 4.4: Consolidated Financial Statements - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 4.4: Consolidated Financial Statements. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: A parent company controls a subsidiary when:
options: It owns more than 50% of the voting rights
It has power over the investee
It is exposed to variable returns from its involvement
Both A and C are correct
questions.correctAnswer: Both A and C are correct
questions.explanation: Control exists when the investor has power over the investee, exposure to variable returns, and the ability to use power to affect returns. Owning >50% of voting rights creates a presumption of control.

questions.question: In consolidation, intra-group balances are:
questions.explanation: All intra-group balances, transactions, income, and expenses are eliminated in full when preparing consolidated financial statements.

questions.question: Unrealized profit on intra-group sales of inventory should be:
options: Recognized in full in the consolidated statements
Eliminated to the extent of the parent's interest
questions.explanation: Unrealized profit resulting from intra-group transactions is eliminated in full. The elimination is allocated between parent and NCI based on their respective interests.

questions.question: Non-controlling interest is presented:
options: As a liability in the consolidated statement of financial position
As a separate component of equity
In the consolidated statement of comprehensive income
In the notes to the financial statements
questions.correctAnswer: As a separate component of equity
questions.explanation: Non-controlling interest is presented within equity in the consolidated statement of financial position, separately from the equity of the owners of the parent.

questions.question: When a parent loses control of a subsidiary, the parent:
options: Derecognizes all assets and liabilities of the subsidiary
Recognizes any retained investment at fair value
Recognizes a gain or loss in profit or loss
questions.explanation: When control is lost, the parent derecognizes the assets and liabilities, measures any retained investment at fair value, and recognizes the difference as a gain or loss in profit or loss.

programs.title: Lesson 5.1: Statement of Cash Flows - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 5.1: Statement of Cash Flows. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: Which of the following is classified as an investing activity?
options: Purchase of property, plant and equipment
questions.correctAnswer: Purchase of property, plant and equipment
questions.explanation: Purchase of PPE is an investing activity as it involves the acquisition of long-term assets used in the business. The other options are financing activities.

questions.question: Under the indirect method, depreciation expense is:
options: Not included in the cash flow statement
questions.explanation: Depreciation is a non-cash expense that was deducted in calculating net profit. Under the indirect method, it is added back to net profit to arrive at cash from operating activities.

questions.question: Interest paid is classified as:
questions.explanation: Interest paid can be classified as either an operating activity or a financing activity. The entity must choose a policy and apply it consistently.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a cash equivalent?
options: Money market fund with 4 months maturity
Bank term deposit with 90 days maturity
questions.correctAnswer: Money market fund with 4 months maturity
questions.explanation: Cash equivalents have maturity of 3 months or less from the date of acquisition. A 4-month maturity exceeds this limit.

questions.question: Dividends received from investments are classified as:
questions.explanation: Dividends received can be classified as either operating or investing activities. The entity must choose a policy and apply it consistently.

programs.title: Lesson 5.2: Financial Ratios and Analysis - Practice Quiz
programs.description: Practice questions for Lesson 5.2: Financial Ratios and Analysis. Test your understanding with 5 questions.

questions.question: The current ratio is calculated as:
questions.explanation: The current ratio measures liquidity by dividing current assets by current liabilities. It indicates the entity's ability to pay short-term obligations.

questions.question: Return on equity (ROE) measures:
questions.explanation: ROE = Net profit ÷ Shareholders' equity. It measures how effectively management is using shareholders' equity to generate profits.

questions.question: An increase in inventory turnover indicates:
questions.explanation: Higher inventory turnover indicates that inventory is being sold more quickly, which is generally favorable as it reduces carrying costs and obsolescence risk.

questions.question: The debt-to-equity ratio of 2.0 means:
options: For every ₱1 of equity, there is ₱2 of debt
For every ₱1 of debt, there is ₱2 of equity
The company has more equity than debt
questions.correctAnswer: For every ₱1 of equity, there is ₱2 of debt
questions.explanation: A debt-to-equity ratio of 2.0 means that debt is twice the equity, indicating higher financial leverage and risk.

questions.question: Which ratio measures the company's ability to pay interest on its debt?
questions.explanation: Interest coverage ratio = EBIT ÷ Interest expense. It measures the company's ability to meet its interest obligations from operating earnings.

programs.title: Board Exam Simulation 1 - Comprehensive Review
programs.description: 100-question timed simulation covering all FAR topics. This simulation mimics the actual CPA Board Exam format and difficulty level.

questions.question: 1. Which qualitative characteristic requires that financial information must be complete, neutral, and free from error?
questions.explanation: Faithful representation requires that financial information be complete, neutral, and free from error.

questions.question: 2. According to the Conceptual Framework, an asset is recognized when:
options: It meets the definition of an asset
It is probable that future economic benefits will flow
Its cost can be measured reliably
questions.explanation: An asset is recognized only when it meets the definition of an asset, is probable to provide future benefits, and has a cost that can be measured reliably.

questions.question: 3. Under PFRS 1, which of the following must be presented in a complete set of financial statements?
questions.explanation: A complete set includes statement of financial position, statement of comprehensive income, statement of changes in equity, statement of cash flows, and notes.

questions.question: 4. In a bank reconciliation, outstanding checks should be:
options: Added to the bank statement balance
Subtracted from the bank statement balance
questions.correctAnswer: Subtracted from the bank statement balance
questions.explanation: Outstanding checks are checks issued by the company but not yet presented to the bank. They should be subtracted from the bank statement balance.

questions.question: 5. When factoring receivables without recourse, the factor assumes:
options: Risk only if the debtor is bankrupt
questions.explanation: Without recourse means the factor assumes the full risk of uncollectibility. The selling company has no further obligation.

questions.question: 6. Under PFRS 2, inventory is measured at:
options: Lower of cost and net realizable value
Higher of cost and net realizable value
questions.correctAnswer: Lower of cost and net realizable value
questions.explanation: Inventories are measured at the lower of cost and net realizable value.

questions.question: 7. Company A uses FIFO. Purchases: Jan 1 - 100 units @ ₱10, Jan 15 - 200 units @ ₱12. Sold 150 units. Cost of goods sold is:
8. Under PFRS 16, which depreciation method allocates a higher amount in earlier years?
questions.explanation: The declining balance method applies a constant percentage to the carrying amount, resulting in higher depreciation in earlier years.

questions.question: 9. An asset costs ₱100,000, has residual value of ₱10,000, and useful life of 5 years. Annual depreciation under straight-line is:
questions.explanation: Straight-line = (Cost - Residual) / Life = (₱100,000 - ₱10,000) / 5 = ₱18,000

questions.question: 10. Internally generated goodwill is:
questions.explanation: Internally generated goodwill is not recognized as an asset. Any costs related to it are expensed as incurred.

questions.question: 11. Recoverable amount is the higher of:
options: Fair value less costs of disposal and value in use
questions.correctAnswer: Fair value less costs of disposal and value in use
questions.explanation: Recoverable amount is the higher of fair value less costs of disposal and value in use.

questions.question: 12. When bonds are issued at a premium, the effective interest rate is:
questions.explanation: When bonds are issued at a premium, investors pay more than face value because the stated rate exceeds market rates.

questions.question: 13. A taxable temporary difference creates:
questions.explanation: Taxable temporary differences will result in taxable amounts in future periods, creating deferred tax liabilities.

questions.question: 14. Under PFRS 15, revenue is recognized when:
questions.explanation: Revenue is recognized when (or as) the entity satisfies a performance obligation by transferring a promised good or service.

questions.question: 15. In consolidated financial statements, intra-group balances are:
questions.explanation: All intra-group balances, transactions, and unrealized profits are eliminated in full when preparing consolidated statements.

questions.question: 16. The functional currency is:
options: The currency of the primary economic environment
questions.correctAnswer: The currency of the primary economic environment
questions.explanation: Functional currency is the currency of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates.

questions.question: 17. Purchase of equipment is classified as:
questions.explanation: Purchase of property, plant and equipment is an investing activity as it involves acquisition of long-term assets.

questions.question: 18. Current assets ÷ Current liabilities equals:
questions.explanation: Current ratio = Current assets / Current liabilities. It measures short-term liquidity.

programs.title: Board Exam Simulation 2 - Advanced Topics
programs.description: 100-question simulation focusing on complex accounting topics including business combinations, consolidated financial statements, and advanced calculations.

questions.question: 1. Goodwill in a business combination is calculated as:
questions.explanation: Goodwill = Consideration transferred + NCI - Net identifiable assets acquired

questions.question: 2. A bargain purchase gain is recognized in:
questions.explanation: A bargain purchase gain is recognized immediately in profit or loss.

questions.question: 3. Non-controlling interest is presented as:
questions.explanation: NCI is presented within equity in the consolidated statement of financial position.

questions.question: 4. Unrealized profit on intra-group inventory sales is eliminated:
options: To the extent of the parent's interest
Only if sold to third parties
questions.explanation: All unrealized profits from intra-group transactions are eliminated in full.

questions.question: 5. Under PFRS 16, a lessee recognizes:
options: A right-of-use asset and lease liability
questions.correctAnswer: A right-of-use asset and lease liability
questions.explanation: PFRS 16 requires lessees to recognize a right-of-use asset and a lease liability for most leases.

questions.question: 6. A convertible bond is classified as:
questions.explanation: Convertible bonds contain both debt and equity components and must be separated.

questions.question: 7. A deductible temporary difference creates:
questions.explanation: Deductible temporary differences result in deductible amounts in future periods, creating deferred tax assets.

questions.question: 8. When translating a foreign operation, equity is translated at:
questions.explanation: Equity items of a foreign operation are translated at historical rates.
